title: Enable Ubuntu tests in CI
body:
Add updates required for ubuntu and run integration tests

Signed-off-by: Brent Baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *baude* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [baude] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@mheon Did some of your pod handling code futher break Ubuntu?
Not that I'm aware of? That should all be OS-agnostic
@edsantiago Looks like your registry script is not working on ubuntu.

This is really strange. Everything points to a difference in the Ubuntu version of BATS: environment variables that are preserved in Fedora/RHEL are being clobbered in Ubuntu. The registry is starting, but subsequent tests aren't getting the right port / testuser / password. Is there some way to find out what version of BATS is being used? It's not listed in the `package_versions` CI step.

I'm also confused about why the `remote` tests are failing: this test is disabled on podman-remote. I think my test for `podman-remote` is failing because of the new APIv2 way. But that's another TODO for me.
Ubuntu:20.04 is shipping `Bats 1.2.0-dev` while F32 is on 1.1.0.
Opened https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/6515. Bats v1.1.0 worked in the Ubuntu tests in the Google cloud.
